And so I've always had this sort of post-sales, you know, how do you operationalize growth through sort of this platform, if you will, in my mind of, you the customer experience, the people experience, and all the handoffs, and then the process and systems and tools layer, and then the analytics layer, if you will.
um, and you guys sound like you've got a lot on your plate.

So my background started in CS.

These were like multi-year engagements, took nine months to stand up, that sort of thing.
So I was the first CS hire and grew that team out to, we acquired another company.

So that was how I had that connection.
And they were looking again for kind of like founding CS.

And how do we think about like scalability and things like that?
And so I wore a lot of hats at Tango for a little while.
I was on the product ops side, just because that feedback loop was so valuable at the seed stage that I ended up like sitting on the product side for a little while.

Being voice of the customer, but also thinking about, like, roadmap prioritization, and, like, I stood up their first support instance, not very well, but, like, sort of just, like, built it out, and this is where the tickets go, this is how we categorize requests, things like that.
Um, and then, yeah, and then they didn't raise their Series B, so, uh, ended up with a small career break, and then that's, uh, I was, growthx found me.
Um, and, yeah, so that's, I mean, that's the short story.
I think, like, all the other stuff is sort of, those are the big points.

Yeah, so, um, I am in a transition period.
I started to oversee, basically, like, within the first month of me joining, we did a reorg, um, and I just ended up being the person that was, like,
Like moving the puzzle pieces and then started managing the director layer, which ended up with me sort of overseeing the full breadth of the delivery org.
I think something we're lacking generally is like a management layer that will allow people to like give feedback, get clarity on an individual basis.
And so I think I've been spread a little thin on that front and yeah, we're going through another reorg now.
The first one was just, I think when I joined, we were transitioning out of air ops and into our atlas, our like our content, our own content platform that Daniel's building.
And so like the cause for the reorg was partly that transition.
They were not going to do a production pause for clients when I joined.
And I was like, please stop production for that week.
And I like convinced everybody that we needed a week of not producing any content so that people could learn the new system and we could do some bug bashing and things like that.
And so I think the other thing that I've been really active in is feedback loops and prioritization for the engineering team.

Not so much on obviously or not at all on the sort of like things that they're building having to do with check that or like the part of the business that is like long term product focused or I guess short term product focused now.
But just in terms of feedback loops for the pieces of the product that are being used on a day to day basis by our internal team, which I think we could reprioritize.
I mean, I think we need more engineering resources.
Generally, I'm just giving a stream of content.

I think we could use a little bit of a reprioritization on engineering resources because there is like in the vision of us turning into a product-led organization and having Atlas be an externally facing offering.
And right now we are very reactive in terms of fixing things on a one-off basis.
There isn't a way to update like pipeline templates and push them through to every content pipeline that exists in the product today, for example.
So every pipeline gets updated as a one-off.
And as we make improvements to the workflows, we have to go back and one-off fix every single pipeline.
And that's just not going to be scalable.
Because we put this in the hands of clients who are expecting kind of an end-to-end experience that continues to improve.

And I, yeah, I think like we, I would love for us to either to make a bit of a decision on like how and set some timelines and structure around how and when we expect to launch Atlas and like what are the milestones there.
But I think we need more product resources as well if we're going to do that.
So can I just summarize that last sort of bit back to you of.
Yeah.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[To understand Growthx's delivery org structure, processes, and challenges.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=239.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Atlas Platform Bottleneck:** The Atlas platform is a key blocker to scalability. Its inability to push global workflow updates forces inefficient, one-off manual fixes for every client pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
  - [**Inconsistent Client Handoffs:** The sales-to-delivery handoff is inconsistent, causing misaligned expectations. Andi is building an MVP client project doc to fix this by next week.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=1191.0)
  - [**Inefficient Production Workflows:** Many editors bypass Atlas for manual work (e.g., in Google Docs), undermining the platform's value and preventing the team from scaling.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=1563.0)
  - [**New Reorg to Force Atlas Adoption:** A new reorg will merge Content Manager and Managing Editor roles, requiring all production staff to own end-to-end content creation for 3–4 accounts by March. This capacity target is designed to force reliance on Atlas for efficiency.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=1682.0)

## Topics

### Atlas Platform Bottleneck

  - [**Problem:** The Atlas platform is not scalable for a product-led future.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
      - [**Root Cause:** It lacks the ability to push global workflow updates.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
      - [**Impact:** Every content pipeline must be updated manually, one-off, which is unsustainable.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
  - [**Resource Misalignment:**](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=633.0)
      - [**Client Ops:** Fixes pipelines one-off but cannot perform root-cause engineering.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=663.0)
      - [**Engineering:** Prioritizes long-term product vision over immediate internal tool improvements.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=495.0)
  - [**Solution:** A clear launch timeline for Atlas as an external product, supported by more product and engineering resources.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=608.0)

### Inconsistent Client Handoffs

  - [**Sales-to-Strategy Sprint Handoff:**](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=1059.0)
      - [**Problem:** No standard process for setting client expectations or defining roles/responsibilities.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=1208.0)
      - [**Impact:** Creates misaligned expectations and stalls production.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=1225.0)
      - [**Andi's MVP Fix:** A client-facing project doc (PowerPoint) with a calendar and weekly deliverables, due next week.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=1250.0)
  - [**Strategy Sprint-to-Pod Handoff:**](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=1380.0)
      - [**Problem:** Inconsistent handoffs cause pods to recalibrate strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=1380.0)
      - [**Impact:** Wastes time and loses context from the initial 8-week sprint.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=1380.0)
      - [**Root Cause:** Inconsistent Atlas training and prompts, plus a lack of visibility into past prompt refinement.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=1488.0)

### Inefficient Production Workflows

  - [**Problem:** Many editors bypass Atlas for manual work (e.g., in Google Docs), undermining the platform's value and preventing the team from scaling.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=1563.0)
  - [**Root Cause:** A mix of personality (tech-averse vs. curious) and inconsistent training.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=1563.0)
  - [**Solution:** A new reorg will merge Content Manager and Managing Editor roles.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=1682.0)
      - [**Goal:** Force reliance on Atlas to meet new capacity targets (3–4 accounts per person by March).](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=1715.0)
      - [**Support:** Panzer holds weekly Atlas troubleshooting sessions, but the technical depth can be off-putting.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=1777.0)

### KPI & Reporting Gaps

  - [**Problem:** Current reporting (clicks, impressions) is not meaningful to clients.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=2018.0)
  - [**Client Focus:** AI mentions and referrals.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=2018.0)
  - [**Solution:** Track AI mentions (quality, not just quantity) and integrate with client CRM data to enable true attribution.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=2018.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Andi:**](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=1250.0)
      - [Launch MVP client project doc (PowerPoint) by next week.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=1250.0)
      - [Use Linear to track director escalations.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=2254.0)
  - [**Ella:**](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=2138.0)
      - [Focus initial work on standardizing the sales-to-delivery handoff.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=2138.0)
      - [Review Linear to understand common escalation types.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=2254.0)
      - [Sync with Andi for feedback on hypotheses.](https://fathom.video/share/61k3uzxWC94x1UEsvQcJG_a919E-vQqr?tab=summary&timestamp=2321.0)
